author: @amikwadze9964
text: I have great respect for Robert, I love his videos, and I found this particular video incredibly helpful.  I just built this model.  It is by Even Erichsen.  This generator is a 3 phase configuration, if you wire it in single phase, it will not function.  The bolts that hold in the magnets are M4x12mm.

author: @FRESNEL_COOKING_SOLAR_OVENS24
text:
To build a hand crank axial flux you must focuse on low rpm. And to do that, you mean to stick to around 60-90rpm. The switching poles must be equal to a motor spinning at 3000rpm. Take 3000:90=number of magnetic poles switches per 1 minute. In this way you are creating a low RPM generator.
And if I am wrong, I would recommend you to have a look at a three phase low rpm generator (90 rpm).
You don't need belt or gearbox. This is inefficient. Instead, you regulate the speed by adding resistance and capacitors with lor pico farads, for example high speed current aluminium capacitors48v. Then you step down to 12v with a micro step down regulator not bigger than 100w.
And this is how you create an actual hand crank generators.
As many poles as possible and you gonna crank much much easier and with less friction.
And if its heavy, you accelerate slowly, of course. And that's better. You don't overcharge.
Use an rpm display to help you manage a gearless generator.
You ve made this video with no actual result. Just an over engineered plastic chasis with belt which seems a waste of time. I can't find any actual inovation on your channel. You are playing around with micro watts for years. Fell sorry for you.

author: @jibberjabber9378
text: Would a compressed helical or fan folded strip of copper work as well or better (or not at all) as the of thin copper wire on a bobbin?  I noticed how thin the wire was and how much of it has to wound to form the coil.  I'm sure there's some sort of relationship between the thickness and number of turns of the wire the magnetism has to pass through to generate x-amount of electron flow.  Wasn't sure if there might be some easier and/or more efficient alternative.  Just a passing thought.

author: @DKFX1
text: Awesome job mate. You are a wellspring of creativity! Not too long ago I built a hand crank generator capable of producing 60 watts by hand using an old hoverboard motor. I 3D printed a simple case and crank handle, then did a bit of rewiring and voila hooked it up to a vice. A fair bit easier than this object you made, but yours look way cooler.

author: @Exevien
text: As you were connecting the first set of magnets, it occured to me to try to connect the rotor and stator to both the bottom and top bolts.   Creating a situation where they rotate opposite of each other.   So both the magnets and the wire sections are rotating, in opposite directions.   I'm curious if that would make it less efficient or more or just nullify it entirely lol.    Even if they needed to be completely mirrored or parallel with each other, I'm curious this effect. Does anybody know?

author: @colleenforrest7936
text:
Ah! So that's what all those years were sitting on your desk in the last video!

It got me wondering if you could create heat from it similar to what was being talked about with a wind generator.  I was thinking like heating water for coffee or soup, or Even a cooker comparable to a hay box or a solar cooker.  Would maybe want to use pedal power instead of a hand crank for anything sustained.  

Basically I was wondering if something that size, powered by a determined human, could generate the amount of heat needed to get those kind of results or would you need a bigger generator?

replies.author: @chuckles1808
replies.text: @whosle Depending on what you mean by "stop", that could be done pretty easily. If you take a transistor or relay and wire it to your display, you could have the screen shut down when pedaling no longer activates the transistor gate. If you meant pauses the game, I think that might require some coding, which might be easy for someone who codes, but I'm not that tech savvy.

author: @mikejones-vd3fg
text: Very cool, will definately give this a try when I get my printer.   The idea of hand cranking  power generation is very neat i wonder why its not as popular for alternative power generatoin im assuming because it takes effort hah? but as a last resort it should be on every offgrider's survival tool list rather than just a gas generator.  15w is still quit a bit when it comes to modern electronics, my usb powerbank charges my laptop at 16 w,  and when in use only uses 8-12w while watching youtubevideos, well under 15 watts  so you could in theory power a laptop with this hand crank generator indefinately.  A pedal bike thing would be even better, then you could get some good exercise also while charging a batter bank when solar is low.    Still wonder how long and hard it would take cranking that thing  to chage a laptop, without that kind of figure its hard to visualize what these hand crank generators can produce and is it worth it?  Maybe with todays power efficient electronics.

replies.author: @DKFX1
replies.text: You'd need to crank it for several hours straight which is pretty unrealistic. Make an adaptor for pedalling with legs and you'll have a much more realistic and efficient crank generator for survival purposes. A bit of rewiring will make it produce 100 watts easy.
